general_note: The world’s first completely organic single malt whisky from the Benromach Distillery in Speyside. Sweet with a little bit of peppery spice.’ MK

Blair Atholl (Pitlochry) 12yo 43% 7. Blair Atholl 1997 43% 9. Blairmhor 8yo 40% 7. Bowmore 12yo 40% ‘Not the most intense Islay malt in terms of peatiness. One of the last to produce its own floor malted barley turned by hand. The 12yo gives you gentle peat smoke with a long and mellow finish.’ MK

Bruichladdich Scottish Barely no statement 50% 9. Bunnahabhain 12yo 40% 7. Bushmills (Irish) 10yo 40% Cambus (grain) 31yo 53.2% 8. Caol Ila 10yo/1999 43% 9. Caol Ila 12yo 43% 7. ‘Lighter than some other Islay malts. Spicy with vanilla hints.’ MK

Cragganmore Distillers Ed. 1987 40% 7. Craigellachie 20yo/1981 43% 7. CU Dhub 40% 7. Dailuaine 16yo 40% 8. Dalmore 12yo 40% ‘One of my personal favourites. The 10yo has a lovely mix of sherry notes with chocolate and orange flavours. I have had the pleasure of meeting the man who makes the vattings and blendings for the Dalmore, Mr Richard Patterson. One of the most charismatic men in the industry.’ MK

A bottle filled from the last cask ever filled at the Dallas Dhu distillery in Forres. Closed in 1983 and reopened in 1988 but only as a historic building. A very special malt from a dearly departed distillery.’ MK
Glenfarclas belongs to a private family owned company somewhat a rarity in the Speyside region these days. A complex malt that is crisp and dry with sweet sherry notes.’ MK
An edition that tried to recapture 19th century bottles that were opened at Macallan.’MK
